validation_spikes.title: ACP + Multica Daemon Integration
validation_spikes.goal: Prove that an ACP-compatible TypeScript agent can register with the Multica daemon, receive a task, and post a response end-to-end without a custom fork of Multica.
done_when: A minimal ACP agent (< 50 LOC) registers with the local Multica daemon
The agent receives a task payload sent via multica CLI
The agent posts a comment back to the triggering issue
No daemon fork, monkey-patching, or undocumented APIs required
ACP SDK version is pinned; adapter layer is defined
validation_spikes.fail_criteria: If registration or message exchange requires changes to the Multica daemon source, stop and escalate

validation_spikes.title: BMad Prompt Portability
validation_spikes.goal: Confirm that core BMad Analyst, PM, and Architect prompts produce useful structured output when invoked via the Anthropic API directly — without IDE, CLAUDE.md, or human guidance during generation.
done_when: Analyst prompt produces a product-brief.md rated >= 4/5 by a developer unfamiliar with the project
PM prompt consumes the brief and produces a PRD with >= 3 testable user stories
Architect prompt consumes the PRD and produces an ADR with >= 1 concrete stack recommendation
All prompts run without modification to core BMad prompt text
validation_spikes.fail_criteria: If prompts require substantial rewriting or output quality < 3/5, escalate scope pivot

features.name: ACP-Compatible Agent Interface
user_stories: As Marco, I want to tag a Multica issue BP and have Ensi automatically start the Analyst stage, so that I get a structured planning session without manually invoking an agent.
acceptance_criteria: Given Ensi is registered in a Multica workspace, when a BP-tagged issue is created, then Ensi receives the task within 10 seconds
Given the Multica daemon is running, when a minimal ACP agent is started, then it appears in multica agent list within 5 seconds
Given an ACP agent is registered, when a task is sent via multica CLI, then the agent receives and processes it without daemon changes

features.name: Analyst Stage — Clarifying Dialogue + product-brief.md
user_stories: As Marco, I want the Analyst to ask me targeted clarifying questions before writing the product brief, so that the brief reflects my specific project context.
As Marco, I want product-brief.md to be saved and committed in my project repo, so that I can review and edit it before the PM stage begins.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Analyst stage has started, when I respond to the first question via a comment, then the Analyst posts the next question or confirms sufficient context
Given the Analyst has asked all questions, when I provide the final answer, then the Analyst generates and commits product-brief.md to .ensi/docs/
Given the Analyst completes, when the artefact is committed, then Ensi posts a comment with a summary and updates the issue status to in_review
The Analyst asks no more than 5 clarifying questions total

features.name: PM Stage — PRD with Testable Acceptance Criteria
user_stories: As Ana, I want the PRD to include testable acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format for every user story, so that dev agents have unambiguous specs.
acceptance_criteria: Given the PM stage receives a product-brief.md, when the PM agent generates the PRD, then prd.md contains >= 3 user stories
Given the PRD is generated, then each user story has a unique ID (US-01, US-02, ...), >= 1 Gherkin acceptance criterion, an effort estimate (S/M/L/XL), and a parent epic reference
Given the PRD is complete, then it includes an explicit MVP scope section with in-scope and out-of-scope lists
Given the PM completes, then prd.md is committed to .ensi/docs/ and a summary comment is posted to the Multica issue

features.name: Architect Stage — Architecture Decision Record
user_stories: As Marco, I want the Architect stage to produce an ADR with documented stack choices and trade-offs, so that I have a written technical decision trail before any coding begins.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Architect stage receives prd.md, when the Architect agent generates the ADR, then architecture.md is written to .ensi/docs/
Given the ADR is generated, then it includes >= 1 architectural decision in ADR format (Context / Decision / Consequences)
Given the ADR is generated, then it explicitly names the technology chosen for each major concern (runtime, storage, LLM interaction, artefact format)
Given the ADR is generated, then rejected alternatives are listed with reasons for rejection

features.name: Schema-Validated Artifact Handoffs
user_stories: As the engineering team, I want each stage to validate its output against a defined schema before advancing, so that downstream agents always receive well-formed input.
acceptance_criteria: Given any stage completes, when the output is generated, then it is validated against the stage's artifact schema before the pipeline advances
Given validation fails, when the retry limit (3) is not exceeded, then the stage retries generation with the validation error as feedback context
Given validation fails after 3 retries, then the session is marked failed, the issue status is updated to blocked, and an error comment is posted

features.name: Human-in-the-Loop Review Gate
user_stories: As Ana, I want the pipeline to pause between stages until my team explicitly approves the artefact, so that we can validate each output before the next agent runs.
acceptance_criteria: Given Ensi has completed a stage, when a team member posts a comment containing LGTM or approve, then Ensi advances to the next stage automatically
Given Ensi is waiting for approval and 72 hours pass, then Ensi posts a reminder comment mentioning the assignee but does not auto-advance
Given a team member posts reject or restart with a reason, then Ensi re-runs the current stage using the rejection reason as additional context

features.name: Session State Persistence and Resumption
user_stories: As Marco, I want to resume a paused or interrupted Ensi session from where it left off, so that I don't lose planning progress if the agent restarts.
acceptance_criteria: Given an Ensi session is in progress and the process is killed, when the daemon restarts Ensi and the same issue is re-triggered, then Ensi reads .ensi/state.json and resumes from the last completed stage
Given Ensi resumes, then it posts a comment confirming resumption and the current stage
Given .ensi/state.json is corrupted or missing, then Ensi posts a comment explaining the issue and offers to restart from the beginning with human confirmation
Given a resume is offered, then Ensi does not silently re-run completed stages

features.name: Zero-Config Onboarding
user_stories: As James, I want to add Ensi to my workspace and run my first session with a single command and no config file editing, so that onboarding is fast enough that I don't give up.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have a Multica workspace and an Anthropic API key, when I run multica agent add ensi and set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, then Ensi is registered and visible in multica agent list within 60 seconds
Given Ensi is registered, then no config file creation or editing is required to trigger a planning session
Given multica agent add ensi is run without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, when the first BP issue is created, then Ensi posts a clear error message with the exact env var name and setup instruction

features.name: Stage Completion Notifications
user_stories: As Ana, I want Ensi to post a comment when each stage completes, so that my team can see planning progress on the Multica board without polling.
acceptance_criteria: Given any pipeline stage completes successfully, when the artefact is committed, then Ensi posts a comment including: which stage completed, a 2-3 sentence artefact summary, the relative path to the artefact file, and instructions for approval/rejection
Given any pipeline stage fails after retries, when the failure is unrecoverable, then Ensi posts a comment explaining the failure in plain language and updates the issue status to blocked

features.name: Pipeline Status Visibility on Multica Board
user_stories: As Ana, I want to see which pipeline stage Ensi is in from the Multica issue view, so that I can track planning progress alongside development work.
acceptance_criteria: Given Ensi is executing any stage, when I view the triggering issue, then the status reflects the current state (in_progress while running, in_review while awaiting approval, blocked on failure, done when all artefacts committed)
Given a stage completes, when I view the issue comments, then the most recent Ensi comment clearly states which stage completed and includes the artefact path

agent_protocol.description: The system must expose an ACP-compatible agent interface that the Multica daemon can discover and register automatically.
The system must receive task payloads from the Multica daemon when a Multica issue is tagged BP (Brainstorm/Planning).
The system must post structured comments to the triggering Multica issue at stage completion, stage failure, and when awaiting human input.
The system must update the Multica issue status to reflect pipeline state (in_progress, in_review, blocked, done).

planning_pipeline.description: The system must execute the Analyst stage first, producing product-brief.md in .ensi/docs/.
The system must execute the PM stage after Analyst completes and human approval is granted, producing prd.md.
The system must execute the Architect stage after PM completes and human approval is granted, producing architecture.md.
Each stage must validate its output against a defined schema before advancing. On validation failure, retry up to 3 times before marking the session failed.
The system must pause between stages and wait for explicit human approval (comment containing LGTM or approve) before advancing.

artifact_management.description: All artefacts must be stored as plain markdown in .ensi/docs/ within the project repo.
The system must commit artefacts to the project repository at each stage boundary.
Artefact filenames must be consistent and predictable — no timestamps or hashes in filenames.

session_state.description: The system must persist session state to .ensi/state.json after every stage transition.
The system must resume a paused or interrupted session from the last successfully completed stage.
The system must support concurrent sessions across different project repos without interference.

onboarding.description: The system must be installable as a Multica agent with a single command (multica agent add ensi). No config file editing required beyond setting ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
The system must display a clear, human-readable error message if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set, with setup instructions.

performance.NFR-01: Analyst stage completion time must be < 5 minutes at p95 for a typical greenfield project brief.
performance.NFR-02: Full pipeline (Analyst + PM + Architect, excluding human review time) must complete in < 20 minutes at p95.
performance.NFR-03: Time from multica agent add ensi to first completed product-brief.md must be < 10 minutes on a clean setup.

reliability.NFR-04: < 5% of sessions must require human restart due to agent error (non-human-caused failures).
reliability.NFR-05: >= 80% of sessions must produce all three artefacts without manual intervention, given human approvals are provided.
reliability.NFR-06: The system must handle Anthropic API transient errors with exponential backoff and a maximum of 3 retries before surfacing the error.

security.NFR-07: API keys must never be written to disk, logs, or artefacts. Read ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from environment variables exclusively.
security.NFR-08: No PII must be stored in artefacts beyond what the user explicitly inputs into the planning session.
security.NFR-09: No telemetry or session content must be transmitted to any third party without explicit user opt-in.

scalability.NFR-10: MVP scope is single-tenant: one Multica workspace, one user's API key.
scalability.NFR-11: The system must support concurrent sessions across multiple project repos without resource contention.

observability.NFR-12: All stage transitions, retries, and errors must be logged to .ensi/logs/ with ISO 8601 timestamps and log levels (INFO, WARN, ERROR).
observability.NFR-13: The Multica issue must always reflect the current pipeline state via status and comments.

compatibility.NFR-14: The system must run on Node.js v20+ on macOS and Linux. No OS-specific native dependencies in MVP.
compatibility.NFR-15: The ACP SDK version must be pinned. The adapter layer must isolate ACP-specific code so a version upgrade does not require changes to the pipeline logic.

non_functional_requirements.deployment: Single binary / single npm install, zero external services beyond Anthropic API and Multica daemon

BriefArtifact.description: Produced by the Analyst agent at the Discovery stage. Captures the validated product brief including personas, risks, and constraints.

PrdArtifact.description: Produced by the PM agent at the Planning stage. Contains functional/non-functional requirements, user stories with Gherkin acceptance criteria, epics, MVP scope, and artifact schemas.

ArchitectureArtifact.description: Produced by the Architect agent at the Architecture stage. Documents the selected tech stack, component design, and Architecture Decision Records (ADRs).

SprintPlanArtifact.description: Produced by the Scrum Master agent at the Development stage. Contains the sprint goal, story breakdown, estimates, and definition of done. Post-MVP (v2).

CodeReviewArtifact.description: Produced by the Code Reviewer agent at the Review stage. Contains a structured list of findings with severity, file/line references, and an overall approval decision.

out_of_scope_v1: Observability stage (OTel traces) — deferred to v2
Agent Memory (vector search) — deferred to v2
Transversal agents beyond Code Reviewer (Tech Writer, Data Engineer, UX Designer) — deferred to v2
Multi-LLM support — only Anthropic API in v1
Brownfield / existing-codebase analysis — requires repo scanning; separate spike needed
Rich artefact UI (web rendering, rich editor) — plain markdown covers MVP needs
Billing / per-token metering — user provides own API key
Multi-tenant / team API key management — single-tenant assumption for MVP
Automated sprint planning from PRD — requires deeper Multica board integration
BMad Developer and QA agent stages — Analyst/PM/Architect pipeline must be validated first
Custom approval keywords — LGTM / approve covers MVP
Slack / email notifications — Multica issue comments are sufficient for MVP

open_questions.question: What exact Multica daemon registration API does an ACP agent use? Is there a published endpoint spec or only convention?
Does multica agent add support a registry / npm-install flow, or does the binary need to be on PATH manually?
What is the approval comment syntax? Should Ensi support natural language approval detection or a strict keyword?
Should the human approval gate be skippable via a config option (e.g., ENSI_AUTO_ADVANCE=true) for automated pipelines?
Is .ensi/state.json safe to commit to a public repo, or should state be gitignored by default?
What is the Multica daemon's behaviour when an ACP agent is unreachable — retry, surface error, or drop?
Are there rate limits on multica issue comment add that could affect stage progress updates during long-running LLM calls?
Should the 72-hour approval timeout be configurable per workspace, per project, or global?
How much prompt adaptation is required to port BMad prompts to work with Anthropic API for general greenfield projects without IDE context?
Does the Architect stage need access to the project's existing repo (git history, package.json) for greenfield, or is the PRD sufficient?
